Standard / Configuration: Production databases must use Multi-AZ configuration

Standard / Configuration: System integrations must implement connection timeouts and retry logic
Acceptable Evidence: Application configuration showing timeout and retry policies
Unacceptable Evidence: Infinite retry loops or no retry strategy

Standard / Configuration: System must implement mechanisms to handle sudden traffic spikes
Acceptable Evidence: Rate limiting configuration or autoscaling policies
Rationale: Protects system stability during unexpected demand

Standard / Configuration: System recovery procedures must be documented and accessible to operations teams
Backup jobs must be monitored and alert on failure
Acceptable Evidence: Backup monitoring dashboards or alert configuration

Standard / Configuration: Backups must be periodically validated through restore testing
Acceptable Evidence: Restore validation reports or test logs
Unacceptable Evidence: Assumption backups are valid without testing

Standard / Configuration: All APIs must require authentication before processing requests
Acceptable Evidence: API gateway configuration or authentication middleware configuration

Standard / Configuration: System must enforce role-based authorization for sensitive operations
Security incidents must be recorded in centralized logging systems
Acceptable Evidence: Security event logs or monitoring dashboards

Standard / Configuration: System vulnerabilities must be tracked and remediated periodically
Acceptable Evidence: Vulnerability scan reports or security remediation tickets

Standard / Configuration: System uptime metrics must be collected and visualized
CPU memory and disk utilization must be monitored for production systems
Alerting systems must include escalation policies for critical incidents
System logs must support search and filtering capabilities
Database query performance must be monitored and analyzed periodically
Acceptable Evidence: Database monitoring dashboards or query logs

Standard / Configuration: System resource usage thresholds must trigger alerts
System must implement caching for frequently accessed data where applicable
Acceptable Evidence: Application architecture documentation or cache configuration
Unacceptable Evidence: Repeated database queries for identical data

Standard / Configuration: Infrastructure changes must be deployed through version-controlled templates
Acceptable Evidence: Infrastructure repository or deployment pipeline logs

Standard / Configuration: All infrastructure changes must be traceable through version control history
Production deployments must require approval before execution
Build artifacts must be stored in a controlled artifact repository
Unacceptable Evidence: Local build artifacts without centralized storage

Standard / Configuration: Critical data must be classified and included in backup policies
Acceptable Evidence: Data classification documentation or backup configuration

Standard / Configuration: System data must follow defined retention policies
Acceptable Evidence: Data retention configuration or policy documentation

Standard / Configuration: Systems must have defined operational ownership
Systems must comply with Bespoke engineering standards policy
